description: Should Scotland be independent? No won 55.3% with record 84.6% turnout. Tests narrowing race with late reversion to status quo after establishment counter-mobilization.

key_events.description: European Parliament elections: UKIP wins across England. Reinforces 'Scotland is different' narrative.
Commonwealth Games in Glasgow. Boosts Scottish national pride.
First TV debate: Salmond vs Darling. Darling wins on currency question.
Second TV debate: Salmond wins audience vote. Yes surges 3-4 points.
YouGov poll shows Yes ahead 51-49. Triggers panic in Westminster. 'The Vow' and Gordon Brown speech.

calibration_notes: Tests narrowing race with late reversion. Yes gained steadily through grassroots mobilization and identity-based campaigning, briefly taking the lead. Establishment counter-mobilization (The Vow, Gordon Brown) pulled undecideds back to No. Tests: status quo bias reasserting under pressure, elite intervention at critical moment, gap between online enthusiasm and final vote, very high turnout bringing disengaged voters who broke for No.
